concerns.text: Task T006a mandates implementing `calculate_l_phys` using the nonsensical placeholder string 'The ratio of the planetary radius...' as the final code formula. This directly violates spec.md FR-012 which requires the specific mathematical formula `6371 / Vsw_mean` and Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) which demands runnable, correct code. This is a semantic weakening of the requirement.

Task T008 describes `circular_block_permutation` with the phrase 'A sufficient number of iterations will be performed'. This silently relaxes spec.md FR-005 which explicitly mandates '10,000 iterations'. The task fails to preserve the specific acceptance criterion for the permutation test count.

Task T009 describes `moving_block_bootstrap` with 'A sufficient number of iterations for 95% confidence intervals'. This silently relaxes spec.md FR-006 which explicitly mandates '1,000 iterations'. The task fails to preserve the specific acceptance criterion for the bootstrap count.

Task T011 implements sensitivity analysis for thresholds `T ∈ {, 500, 600}`, omitting the first value `400`. This violates spec.md FR-007 and US-3 which explicitly require the set `T ∈ {400, 500, 600} km s⁻¹`. The acceptance criterion for the full threshold sweep is dropped.

Phase 7 (Tasks T045-T050) introduces Alfvén speed calculations, reference frame transformations, and schematic diagrams. These capabilities are not authorized by spec.md (which limits scope to linear correlation, permutation tests, and a fixed heuristic threshold) and contradict the 'Scope Alignment' note in tasks.md itself. This constitutes unauthorized scope creep and a violation of the project's defined boundaries.

The independent test description for User Story 2 contains the placeholder 'The true lag is approximately one hour.' instead of the specific synthetic dataset condition defined in spec.md US-2 (true lag of 45 min). This undermines the testability of the success criterion and drops the specific acceptance scenario.
